specific_location: The bird was found in the portion of the Julesburg SWA just South of Ovid, CO along Sedgwick County Rd. 29. and was 50 yards East of the South Platte bridge on the South side of the river.
description: This bird was the overall size and shape of a Yellow-billed Cuckoo. The bird was about 12 in. long. Upper parts: The crown, back, upper wings and upper tail were generally a uniform, grayish brown. Bill: Noticeably curved, sharp and all black. Face: Grayish with a red orbital ring clearly visible around the eye. Under parts: Throat, breast and belly clean white. Tail: Long and graduated. Grayish brown above slightly tipped in white (seen when tail was spread). Grayish brown below with small, white ends to the feathersBehaviors: The bird was very curious at my whistled imitations of it’s call and came out into the open for some excellent views. Most of the time, however, it stayed in the lower branches of cottonwoods or in the under story.Call: A repeated “cu cu cu” along with other occasional guttural croaking calls.Plumage: adult
